title: rsac is the ground-truth capture API; downstreams adapt to it
rationale: rsac is a cross-platform audio CAPTURE library (system/device/app/process-tree; WASAPI/PipeWire/CoreAudio; rtrb SPSC bridge). GOAL: design the BEST-possible, most performant + feature-rich capture API (helpers, macros, types) — rsac DEFINES the UX that downstreams consume; it is NOT retrofitted to any one consumer. audio-graph (../audio-graph) is one EXAMPLE consumer whose pain points are EVIDENCE of missing affordances, not a spec. Scope stays capture-only per VISION.md (no DSP/mixing/encoding). RT callback thread NEVER alloc/lock/block. Module DAG core->bridge->audio->api. Verify tiers: Windows-native, WSL (feat_linux), Go (rsac-ffi staticlib), macOS via GH Actions CI. Commits: no Co-Authored-By/byline. Every non-fixed review comment -> tracking issue.

name: verification toolchain + os-eco coordination
description: How to verify changes across platforms and coordinate workflows via seeds/mulch

name: goal backlog: 56 seeds tasks across 6 waves
description: The decomposed executable backlog for the rsac goal, loaded into seeds

name: binding crates must select rsac backend per-target
description: rsac gates platform backends behind feat_windows/feat_linux/feat_macos; enabling the wrong one on a host does not compile

title: Bridge counters + producing-state on CapturingStream trait
rationale: Prerequisite for stream_stats() and windowed backpressure; exposes counters BridgeShared already maintains through the public trait with default-0 bodies so all existing impls/mocks keep compiling.

title: AudioBuffer metering added in core/buffer.rs; call-site refactor deferred to file owners
rationale: STRICT area boundary: this wave-1 agent owns only src/core/buffer.rs, so it cannot touch src/main.rs / src/bin/pipewire_test.rs / rsac-python even though rsac-9a18 acceptance criteria require deleting their local meters.

title: CaptureTarget canonical string grammar for FromStr/TryFrom/Display round-trip
rationale: Downstream waves (bindings, CLI arg parsing) need a single canonical, lossless string form for CaptureTarget. Chose a scheme:body grammar with first-colon-only split so device ids with colons survive, and AudioError::InvalidParameter to match existing api.rs error style.

name: Public read-path types must not depend on cfg-gated imports
description: api.rs gated AudioFormat import to Linux warning-cleanliness, but format() needs it everywhere

name: bridge pop() allocates user copy by design; alloc-free guarantee is producer-only
description: BridgeConsumer::pop does original.clone() (ring_buffer.rs:463) allocating a fresh user Vec every call; this is intentional (non-RT consumer thread). ADR-0001 alloc-free guarantee covers ONLY BridgeProducer::push_samples_or_drop. Any alloc-counting test/bench MUST exclude pop() from the measured region or it will see one alloc per pop.

name: Wave-1 gotchas: doc-link + global-allocator test parallelism
description: Two CI-breaking patterns Wave-1 review caught; avoid in later waves

title: UserFacingError carries both kind and backend_code
rationale: Orchestrator brief and seed spec disagreed on the field set; superset of both is non-breaking thanks to #[non_exhaustive], satisfies all acceptance criteria, and gives UIs both the high-level category and the raw OS code.

name: wasapi 0.23 get_device replaces hand-rolled collection scan
description: Wave-2 rsac-b546: wasapi 0.23 bump simplifications

title: AudioDevice::kind() added as fallible provided trait method
rationale: Additive/non-breaking: provided method so external impls keep compiling and inherit PlatformNotSupported until they override. Fallible (AudioResult<DeviceKind>) resolves M1 api-fit clash and lets CoreAudio return Err when scope is indeterminate. Windows delegates to existing IMMEndpoint::GetDataFlow probe.

name: napi-Float32Array-zero-widen
description: NAPI AudioChunk delivers f32 via Float32Array, not Vec<f64>

name: py-to_bytes-le-no-30
description: Python to_bytes() uses to_le_bytes loop, no from_raw_parts (#30)

title: BackpressureReport canonical-defined in introspection.rs; lib.rs re-export + windowed wiring are cross-area follow-ups
rationale: stats-observability owns ONLY introspection.rs + api.rs; the lib.rs re-export and the CapturingStream/BridgeStream windowed override live in files owned by other areas, so this area shipped the type + a read-side lifetime fallback and recorded the wiring as follow-ups.

title: Order C-layer callback clear before cgo.Handle.Delete to fix #28 Go UAF
rationale: cgo.Handle.Value() panics if the handle was Deleted; a callback in-flight on the FFI delivery thread during Close() could resolve a just-deleted handle. Tearing down the C delivery path (set_callback NULL + capture_free) before Delete, plus a top-level recover in goAudioCallback, converts the residual race from a process-aborting UAF into a harmlessly dropped delivery.

name: binding-feature-preset-and-version-lockstep
description: ci-and-presets area: canonical per-target backend selection convention (docs/features.md) + versioning/ABI lockstep contract (RELEASE_PROCESS.md/CHANGELOG/ci.yml)

name: bridge pop now moves ring buffer; recycles a pooled spare (no clone/memcpy)
description: rsac-17d1 changed BridgeConsumer::pop: it MOVES the ring's AudioBuffer to the user (no original.clone()) and recycles a SPARE Vec (from a consumer-side spare_pool, or freshly allocated off-RT when the pool drains) back to the producer free-list. Net: per-pop full-buffer memcpy eliminated; consumer alloc count is equal-or-better (alloc, no copy). Supersedes the framing in mx-c81d32: pop no longer clones, but may still allocate one spare Vec per pop once the seeded spare_pool empties (off-RT, by design). ADR-0001 producer alloc-free guarantee unchanged. Any alloc-probe must still exclude pop().

name: bridge-zerocopy SampleRing: f32 ring + parallel metadata sidecar, all-or-nothing chunk
description: rsac-3616 added feature bridge-zerocopy (default OFF) + create_sample_ring/SampleRingProducer/SampleRingConsumer in ring_buffer.rs. Sample ring is rtrb::RingBuffer<f32> written via write_chunk_uninit + CopyToUninit (copy_to_uninit on the two wrapped MaybeUninit slices) + unsafe commit_all — zero Vec/AudioBuffer alloc on producer. A parallel rtrb::RingBuffer<ChunkMeta> sidecar carries (len,channels,sample_rate,timestamp_nanos; u64::MAX = no-ts). Producer checks BOTH rings have room before committing so a chunk is atomic (consumer never sees partial). Consumer reads meta, read_chunk(len).as_slices() -> extend_from_slice -> commit_all, reconstructs AudioBuffer (user Vec alloc on consumer thread). rtrb bumped 0.3.3->0.3.4 (purely additive).

title: Windowed drop-rate atomics live in BridgeShared; BackpressureReport type + api wiring is the stats area's job
rationale: bridge-internals owns only ring_buffer.rs/stream.rs; the BackpressureReport type lives in core/introspection.rs and the trait/AudioCapture wiring in api.rs, both owned by the stats-area agent. So bridge-internals exposes the RT atomics + read API and stops there to avoid editing unowned files.

title: push_samples_guarded (catch_unwind) provided as producer method; FFI call-site wiring deferred to platform agents
rationale: rsac-d0ba acceptance allows 'the producer method itself' to be the guarded path. The macos/windows/linux thread.rs call sites are owned by platform agents, so bridge-internals provides+tests the guarded method and leaves the call-site swap as a follow-up rather than editing unowned files.

name: Wave-2 gotchas: MSRV vs is_multiple_of, doc-link classes, Rc-move-in-closure
description: Recurring CI-breakers from Wave 2 for later waves to pre-empt

name: DeviceInfo snapshot + infallible describe() with Input fallback
description: rsac-a481 (wave3, device-metadata-core): Added #[non_exhaustive] pub struct DeviceInfo{id:DeviceId,name:String,kind:DeviceKind,is_default:bool,default_format:Option<AudioFormat>} in src/core/interface.rs + provided AudioDevice::describe(&self)->DeviceInfo composing existing id()/name()/is_default()/kind()/supported_formats(). KEY: describe() is INFALLIBLE but kind() is fallible — describe falls back to DeviceKind::Input (capture-only default) when kind() errors (PlatformNotSupported default). default_format = supported_formats().into_iter().next() (None on Linux/PipeWire by design, L2). Added kind:Option<DeviceKind> to AudioSourceKind::Device in introspection.rs, populated via dev.kind().ok(); to_capture_target match already uses .. so unaffected. NOTE: AudioSourceKind is NOT #[non_exhaustive] so adding the Device field is technically breaking for external exhaustive matchers — only in-crate introspection.rs sites + tests/ci_audio/process_tree.rs (Application/SystemDefault only) touch it, so crate builds clean. FOLLOW-UP: lib.rs re-export of DeviceInfo deferred to that file's owner (precedent mx-819c89); meanwhile reachable via crate::core::interface::DeviceInfo.

name: Linux native PipeWire APPLICATION enumeration (rsac-8ebb / H4 part 2)
description: Wave-3 rsac-8ebb added native in-process app enumeration to replace the pw-dump scrape in core/introspection.rs::list_audio_applications_into. thread.rs: new pub(crate) PwAppSnapshot{pid,app_name,node_serial}; RegistrySnapshot gained applications:BTreeMap<u32 global_id,PwAppSnapshot>; the EXISTING registry global closure ObjectType::Node arm now branches: Audio/Sink|Audio/Source -> devices (unchanged) else media.class contains 'Stream' + parseable non-zero application.process.id -> applications, REUSING the already-MOVED snapshot Rc so NO new sibling clone is needed (respects Rc-move-into-closure gotcha mx-3f281e). global_remove now removes from BOTH maps. New PipeWireCommand::SnapshotApplications + PipeWireThread::snapshot_applications() runs the same run_snapshot_roundtrip() sync/done settle as SnapshotDevices, then PID-dedups when building the owned Vec (first-node-per-PID wins via BTreeMap ascending global-id order). mod.rs: pub struct LinuxApplicationInfo{process_id,name,node_serial} + pub fn enumerate_audio_applications() (feat_linux-gated) spawning a short-lived PipeWireThread — the crate::audio::linux sibling of macos::enumerate_audio_applications / windows::enumerate_application_audio_sessions. GOTCHA: cross-platform doc links to crate::audio::macos/windows items MUST be plain backticks not intra-doc links (those modules are target_os-gated; broken links fail the --all-features docs.rs gate). FOLLOW-UP (NOT in owned files): introspection.rs:209-269 Linux arm still calls pw-dump — its owner must switch the native path to crate::audio::linux::enumerate_audio_applications() with pw-dump kept as cfg fallback (DAG: core reaches it via crate::audio facade, same precedent as get_device_enumerator at introspection.rs:138).

name: rsac_event!/rsac_span! shim resolves level per-arm, never via a normalizer macro in arg position
description: src/trace.rs (rsac-624c): #[macro_export] rsac_event!/rsac_span! expand to tracing::event!/span! under feature=tracing and fall back to log:: when off. GOTCHA: macro_rules does NOT eagerly expand a helper macro in argument position, so you canNOT write rsac_event!(@arm $crate::__rsac_level!($lvl), ...) and have it match $lvl:ident (it sees literal tokens '$crate :: __rsac_level ! (info)'). FIX: one public arm per literal level token (error/warn/info/debug/trace) forwarding BOTH the resolved tracing Level ident (ERROR/WARN/INFO/DEBUG/TRACE) and the log macro ident to an internal @__pick arm that uses #[cfg(feature=tracing)]/#[cfg(not)] inner blocks. Naming $crate::__rsac_tracing (a module only compiled under the feature) inside the cfg-gated block is fine: cfg-stripping discards the statement before its inner macro path is name-resolved. log fallback flattens field=value via a String (fmt::Write) on the NON-RT control plane only. Call sites must use portable field=value form; tracing %/? sigils do not exist in the log fallback. install_default_tracing() is #[cfg(feature=tracing)], best-effort/idempotent via set_global_default->bool. Feature: tracing=[dep:tracing], tracing 0.1.41 optional. mod trace declared before utils in lib.rs; macros land at crate root.

name: Wave-3 builder-ergonomics api.rs additions (target_str/preflight/RunningCapture)
description: rsac-0f75/9175/b65a landed in src/api.rs ONLY (additive). (1) target_str(&str)->AudioResult<Self> + infallible try_target_str(&str)->Self parse via CaptureTarget::from_str (needs 'use std::str::FromStr'); errors are AudioError::InvalidParameter{param:capture_target}. (2) preflight(&self)->AudioResult<()> extracts build()'s device-independent checks (caps gate + 6-rate whitelist [22050,32000,44100,48000,88200,96000] + channels 1..=32); SUPPORTED_SAMPLE_RATES/MAX_CHANNELS promoted from build()-local consts to MODULE-level consts so preflight+tests share them; build() now calls self.preflight()? first (behavior-preserving, same error variants/order caps->rate->channels). (3) RunningCapture(AudioCapture) RAII guard: builder.start(self)->AudioResult<RunningCapture>=build()?+capture.start()?; Deref/DerefMut to AudioCapture; Drop calls self.0.stop() (idempotent, no double-stop since AudioCapture::stop takes the Arc then AudioCapture::Drop is no-op); into_inner() uses ManuallyDrop+ptr::read to move out WITHOUT running guard Drop (sound: single field read once). GOTCHA: api.rs does NOT import ProcessId/ApplicationId/DeviceId at module scope and test mod only imports AudioFormat,SampleFormat -> qualify crate::core::config::ProcessId etc in tests. RunningCapture is pub in api but NOT re-exported from lib.rs/prelude.rs (other-area files); reachable as rsac::api::RunningCapture; re-export is a cross-area follow-up. Mock gained stop_calls:AtomicU64+stop_calls() to assert stop-once/no-double-stop.

name: CachePadded newtype + period-capacity helper in ring_buffer.rs (wave 3)
description: rsac-9348: added pub(crate) CachePadded<T>(#[repr(align(64))] + Deref/DerefMut) and CACHE_LINE_BYTES=64. Wrapped BridgeShared producer-hot counters (buffers_pushed/buffers_dropped/consecutive_drops) AND the lone consumer-hot buffers_popped each in CachePadded so they sit on distinct 64B lines (no false sharing). KEY: Deref makes all existing self.field.load/.fetch_add/.store call sites compile UNCHANGED (api.rs/stream.rs/interface.rs only read via .load()). Both constructors (create_bridge_with_options + create_sample_ring) now use CachePadded::new(AtomicU64::new(0)). repr(align(..)) needs a LITERAL so 64 is hard-coded with a test asserting CACHE_LINE_BYTES==64. rsac-b655: pure pub fn calculate_capacity_for_period(period_frames, channels)->usize: scale=ceil(1024/period_frames).max(1), raw=12*scale, clamp[8,1024], next_power_of_two; fallback 64 when period_frames==0||channels==0. channels is in the brief-mandated signature but intentionally unused (let _ = channels) since a ring slot holds the whole interleaved period regardless of width. DOC-LINK GOTCHA reconfirmed: the PUBLIC calculate_capacity_for_period doc uses PLAIN BACKTICKS (not [X] intra-doc links) for pub(crate) consts or docs.rs --all-features fails private_intra_doc_links; pub(crate)->pub(crate) links are fine. Deferred (not owned files): SPSC contention bench in benches/bridge.rs + backend wiring (WASAPI GetBufferSize / PipeWire / CoreAudio IOProc).

name: macOS multi-format probe + audio-app filter (rsac-81ae/84fd)
description: coreaudio.rs supported_formats() now probes ALL output streams: query kAudioDevicePropertyStreams (output scope) for AudioStreamID list, then per-stream kAudioStreamPropertyAvailableVirtualFormats which returns an array of AudioStreamRangedDescription (NOT bare ASBD) = {mFormat: ASBD, mSampleRateRange: AudioValueRange} = 56 bytes (40+16). Read .mFormat -> existing asbd_to_audio_format (skips non-PCM/big-endian, never errors). Current format first, dedup, zero-stream device -> vec![]. enumerate_audio_applications() now FILTERS to live audio PIDs on macOS 14.4+ (gate via crate::core::capabilities::get_macos_version()); new enumerate_audio_applications_all() = unfiltered diagnostics + <14.4/empty-intersection fallback. introspection.rs list_audio_applications_into needs NO change (same fn name). FourCC selectors NOT reliably exported by coreaudio-sys 0.2.17 -> define as VERIFIED hex consts: kAudioHardwarePropertyProcessObjectList prs#=0x70727323, kAudioProcessPropertyPID ppid=0x70706964, kAudioProcessPropertyIsRunningOutput piro=0x7069726f (NOT pruo!), kAudioProcessPropertyIsRunning pir?=0x7069723f, kAudioStreamPropertyAvailableVirtualFormats sfma=0x73666d61, kAudioDevicePropertyStreams stm#. CoreAudio create-vs-get rule: AudioObjectGetPropertyData fills POD buffers you do NOT own -> no CFRelease. audio->core dep (get_macos_version) is DAG-legal.

lib TEST target fails to compile: src/trace.rs:231 'local ambiguity when calling macro rsac_event: multiple parsing options: built-in NTs tt (msg) or ident (field)'. A rsac_event! macro_rules arm matches $field:ident in a position ambiguous with the $msg tt/expr fragment. This blocks ALL 'cargo test --lib' (test cfg pulls trace.rs), while 'cargo build --lib' and 'cargo doc --all-features' succeed. trace.rs is an untracked cross-area shim.
resolution: Reviewers/agents verifying core (non-trace) changes must fall back to 'cargo build --lib' + 'cargo doc --no-deps --lib --all-features' + close-read of #[cfg(test)] modules, since the test binary cannot link. Actual fix belongs to the trace.rs structured-instrumentation area: reorder/disambiguate the macro arm (e.g. require key=value via tt-muncher or a literal separator before the ident) so ident no longer competes with the message fragment.

description: macro_export macro_rules referencing helper shim modules via $crate::__rsac_X fails to resolve when those modules are declared as 'pub mod __rsac_X' INSIDE a submodule (e.g. src/trace.rs). A #[macro_export] macro's $crate resolves to the crate root (rsac::), but the module actually lives at rsac::trace::__rsac_X. Symptom: E0433 'cannot find __rsac_tracing in $crate'. Critically, this only surfaces when the macro is actually EXPANDED (cargo build --features tracing, or cargo test which expands it in tests) -- a plain default 'cargo build' passes because the macro is never invoked in non-test lib code, giving a false green.
resolution: Reference the shims as $crate::trace::__rsac_X (their real path) OR add crate-root re-exports in lib.rs (pub use crate::trace::{__rsac_log, ...}). Always verify a #[macro_export] macro with 'cargo test' AND with every feature that changes its expansion (cargo build --features tracing), never just default cargo build.

description: Two macro_rules arms '($($field:ident = $val:expr),+ $(,)?)' (fields only) and '($($field:ident = $val:expr),+ , $($msg:tt)+)' (fields + trailing message) are ambiguous. rustc: 'local ambiguity when calling macro: multiple parsing options: built-in NTs tt (msg) or ident (field)'. After parsing 'expr ,' the parser cannot decide whether the next token starts another field-ident repetition or the $msg:tt run. Hit in src/trace.rs rsac_event! @__l arms.
resolution: Disambiguate the field/message split with a non-overlapping marker token instead of relying on ident-vs-tt after a comma: e.g. require call sites to separate the message with an explicit internal delimiter arm, parse fields with a tt-muncher accumulating into an @acc list then a distinct terminal arm for the message, or front the message with a forced string-literal matcher. Verify with cargo test under default features.

description: A #[macro_export] macro expands at the downstream call site, so it must reference helpers via absolute $crate:: paths. When the helper shim modules (e.g. __rsac_log/__rsac_tracing/__rsac_alloc) live INSIDE a submodule like 'mod trace', the path is $crate::trace::__rsac_log NOT $crate::__rsac_log. The wrong (crate-root) path fails E0433 'cannot find X in $crate' ONLY when the arm using it is actually exercised, so a feature-gated arm hides the bug until you build --features <that>.
resolution: Fully-qualify through the owning module ($crate::trace::__rsac_X), OR re-export the shims at the crate root with #[doc(hidden)] pub use. Verify BOTH feature on AND off (cargo build --lib AND cargo build --lib --features tracing, plus cargo test --lib for the feature-off lib-test target).

description: A macro arm like ($($f:ident = $v:expr),+ , $($msg:tt)+) fails with 'local ambiguity: built-in NTs tt vs ident' because ',' is in FOLLOW(expr) so the matcher cannot tell a continuing field from the message tail. This blocks the WHOLE lib-test target, even feature-off.
resolution: Rewrite as a tt-muncher: peel one 'field = val ,' pair at a time into a bracketed accumulator [ ... ] via a recursive @munch arm; terminal arms split empty-acc (message only) vs non-empty-acc (fields + message). Arm order matters: peel-more, then peel-last (lone field = val with optional trailing comma), then the two message terminals.

name: rustdoc-link-lints-are-cfg-gated-per-host
description: rustdoc strips #[cfg(target_os=X)] items BEFORE intra-doc-link resolution, so a broken/redundant/private-target link inside a windows-only or macos-only module is invisible when running the docs gate on Linux/WSL (and vice-versa). docs.rs renders all 3 targets per [package.metadata.docs.rs].targets, so it WILL catch them at publish time. MITIGATION: run RUSTDOCFLAGS='--cfg docsrs -D warnings' cargo doc --no-deps --all-features on BOTH a Windows host and WSL to cover every platform module. The lint classes that recur: redundant-explicit-links (drop the explicit (path) when the bare label resolves), private-intra-doc-links (pub(crate) target from public docs -> use plain backticks), broken links to methods (need Self:: qualification).

macOS CoreAudio backend code (src/audio/macos/*) is #[cfg(target_os=macos)]-gated so it CANNOT be compiled on the Windows/Linux/WSL dev tiers — clippy/build errors there only surface on macOS CI (Lint & Format feat_macos), which fail-fasts and cancels the rest of the matrix. A Wave-3 enumeration test called device.current_stream_format() on the Box<dyn AudioDevice> returned by default_device(), but current_stream_format() is a PRIVATE INHERENT method on the concrete MacosAudioDevice, not on the AudioDevice trait — unreachable through the trait object (E0599).
resolution: For macOS-only changes, treat macOS CI as the compile gate and expect a round-trip. Inherent methods on a concrete backend type are NOT callable through Box<dyn AudioDevice>; either add the method to the trait (with a default) or test it via the concrete type / a sibling unit test. After pushing macOS-only fixes, watch the feat_macos lint job specifically.

name: default-device-kind-only-overridden-on-windows
description: As of wave-4, AudioDevice::kind() is overridden ONLY by WASAPI (windows/wasapi.rs:542 via IMMEndpoint::GetDataFlow). LinuxAudioDevice (audio/linux/mod.rs) and MacosAudioDevice (audio/macos) do NOT override it, so they inherit the trait-default Err(PlatformNotSupported) and describe() falls back to DeviceKind::Input. Any cross-platform test asserting 'default device kind() is Ok' (sd rsac-1d02 criterion c) MUST gate that hard-Ok assertion to cfg(target_os=windows); on linux/macos assert the honest PlatformNotSupported + Input-fallback instead, or it false-fails on a real Linux/macOS audio runner.

name: go-target-string-via-ffi-not-reimplemented
description: Wave4 rsac-28ee/fe6e/fd16 (ffi-go-propagation). (1) String-target convenience: added FFI rsac_builder_set_target_str(RsacBuilder*, const char* spec) that routes through AudioCaptureBuilder::target_str (parse-then-commit via CaptureTarget::from_str; bad string -> AudioError::InvalidParameter -> RSAC_ERROR_INVALID_PARAMETER; builder unchanged on failure). Go side does NOT reimplement the grammar: ParseTarget(spec) validates by routing through a throwaway C builder and returns CaptureTarget{kind:targetString,spec}; new targetString kind carried into Build switch -> rsac_builder_set_target_str. Added CaptureBuilder.SetTargetString(spec)error (fail-fast) + WithTargetString(spec) (fluent, defers error to Build). (2) stream_stats/format: FFI rsac_capture_stream_stats/rsac_capture_format ALREADY existed; Go added AudioCapture.StreamStats()(StreamStats,error) + Format()(AudioFormat,error) marshalling repr(C) RsacStreamStats/RsacAudioFormat out-params; new exported StreamStats + AudioFormat + SampleFormat(int, C.RSAC_SAMPLE_FORMAT_* discriminants, String()) structs. (3) Buffer metering: FFI rsac_audio_buffer_rms/_peak/_rms_dbfs/_peak_dbfs(const RsacAudioBuffer*)->float wrap AudioBuffer's methods (null rms/peak->0.0, null dbfs->NEG_INFINITY). Go AudioBuffer is fully Go-owned (C buffer freed in audioBufferFromC) so Go RMS/Peak/RMSDbfs/PeakDbfs are reimplemented in pure Go over b.data, EXACTLY mirroring core lin_to_dbfs (level<=0 -> -Inf else 20*log10) and f64-accumulated NaN-safe reduction. GOTCHAS: declare every new fn in BOTH bindings/rsac-ffi/include/rsac.h AND bindings/rsac-go/rsac.h (lockstep). FFI doc-links: AudioCaptureBuilder/AudioBuffer/CaptureTarget are all imported at lib.rs:25 so use BARE intra-doc labels [AudioCaptureBuilder::target_str]; an explicit [label](rsac::path) duplicate trips redundant-explicit-links under the docs.rs -D warnings gate. gofmt re-aligns contiguous struct-field runs (a comment splits a run) - adding a field forces re-tab of the whole CaptureTarget block. ptr::dangling_mut stabilized 1.84 (MSRV 1.87 ok) for the null-builder FFI test.

A #[macro_export] macro_rules! capture! built as a tt-muncher used an internal '@munch ($builder:expr) <key>: <val>' accumulator plus a public entry arm '($($rest:tt)+) => { $crate::capture!(@munch (Builder::new()) $($rest)+) }'. With the entry arm placed FIRST, '($($rest:tt)+)' matched EVERY token stream including the internal '@munch (..) ..' recursive calls, re-wrapping them forever -> 'error: recursion limit reached while expanding $crate::capture!'. rustc tries arms top-to-bottom.
resolution: Place the specific internal '@munch ...' arms (terminal + each key arm) BEFORE the generic public '() ' and '($($rest:tt)+)' entry arms. Then the @munch arms win for internal calls and the catch-all only fires for real user input. Verified via 'cargo test --doc' (external-crate expansion proves $crate::api/$crate::core::config paths) + 'cargo test --lib' unit module.

name: pyo3-async-cm-completed-awaitable-and-del-finalizer
description: rsac-python (rsac-4779): PyO3 0.24 AudioCapture async context-manager + finalizer. __aenter__/__aexit__ do the (non-blocking) start/stop synchronously and return a tiny CompletedAwaitable pyclass implementing __await__/__iter__(slf)->slf + __next__ that raises PyStopIteration::new_err(value); awaiting an already-complete coroutine yields nothing and stops immediately with the stored value, so NO async runtime / pyo3-async-runtimes / asyncio dep is needed. __aexit__ swallows close() errors ONLY when exc_type.is_some() (an exception is already propagating) so teardown never masks the body exception; with no exception in flight a close failure is surfaced. __del__(&self, py) is a plain #[pymethods] fn (PyO3 has no special slot for it but CPython wires tp_finalize from the type dict) that best-effort take()s the inner Mutex<Option<AudioCapture>> and stops under py.allow_threads, swallowing ALL errors incl poisoned lock -> never panics/raises, idempotent vs close()/with/async with. Construct CompletedAwaitable via Py::new returning PyResult (propagate with ?, do NOT .expect/panic). slf.borrow(py).start(py)? then slf.into_any() is fine (immutable borrow dropped before the consuming into_any).

name: SampleFormat-no-Display-map-explicitly
description: rsac::SampleFormat (re-exported at crate root) derives Debug/Clone/Copy/PartialEq/Eq/Hash but has NO Display impl (only bits_per_sample()->u16). Bindings exposing format().sample_format as a string must map variants explicitly (I16/I24/I32/F32) rather than rely on Display; rsac-python uses a private fn sample_format_name(SampleFormat)->&'static str returning lowercase 'i16'/'i24'/'i32'/'f32' with an exhaustive match (a new variant forces an update; covered by a unit test). StreamStats (re-exported at crate root) is #[non_exhaustive] + derives Default; reading its public fields (overruns/buffers_captured/buffers_dropped/buffers_pushed/uptime:Duration/is_running/format_description) from another crate is allowed (only construction + exhaustive match are gated), and StreamStats::default() gives an all-zero/is_running=false snapshot for the no-stream case. AudioCapture::format()->Option<AudioFormat> and stream_stats()->StreamStats both take &self.

name: Linux supported_formats via EnumNodeFormats (rsac-7469/PR-5)
description: thread.rs: new PipeWireCommand::EnumNodeFormats{serial,response_tx:AudioResult<Vec<crate::core::config::AudioFormat>>} + pub fn enum_node_formats(&self,serial:&str) accessor (SNAPSHOT_TIMEOUT-bounded, mirrors snapshot_devices). Handler in pw_thread_main: run_snapshot_roundtrip to settle registry, invert snapshot.devices (BTreeMap<global_id,PwDeviceSnapshot> where .id=object.serial) to find registry global id for the serial, registry.bind::<pipewire::node::Node,_>(&GlobalObject{id:global_id,type_:ObjectType::Node,...}), register node.add_listener_local().param(|seq,param_type,index,next,param|) where param callback sig is Fn(i32,ParamType,u32,u32,Option<&Pod>); compare param_type.as_raw()!=ParamType::EnumFormat.as_raw() (avoid relying on ParamType:PartialEq), format_utils::parse_format must be (Audio,Raw), reuse AudioInfoRaw::new()+parse(param) (pulls default out of SPA choice), then map info.format(). node.enum_params(0,Some(ParamType::EnumFormat),0,u32::MAX) then a core.sync(0)/done pump_sync roundtrip (bounded SNAPSHOT_TIMEOUT) so param events settle before reading the Rc<RefCell<Vec>>. CRITICAL: libspa 0.9.2 AudioFormat is a STRUCT (newtype over spa_audio_format) with associated consts + derives Copy/Clone/PartialEq/Eq -> compare with ==; NO plain F32 const (only F32LE/F32BE/F32P), but S16/S24/S32 host-native aliases DO exist. Map S16/S16LE->I16, S24/S24LE/S24_32LE->I24, S32/S32LE->I32, F32LE->F32, else None; skip rate==0||channels==0. ADVISORY ONLY (L2/EF-3): never alters param_changed connect-time negotiation/delivered AudioBuffer.format(). Unknown serial / bind fail / no daemon -> Ok(vec![]). mod.rs supported_formats() spawns short-lived PipeWireThread, enum_node_formats(&self.id), .inspect_err+.unwrap_or_default (feat_linux) else Vec::new(); thread mod is wholly feat_linux-gated so helper+tests are implicitly gated. PipeWireThread ref in supported_formats doc must be PLAIN backticks (pub(crate)).

name: napi-object-no-methods-and-fixed-index-js-reexport
description: rsac-napi (bindings/rsac-napi): #[napi(object)] structs (AudioChunk, JsStreamStats, JsAudioFormat) become PLAIN JS objects and canNOT carry callable methods - only fields. So per-element/parameterized accessors (e.g. AudioBuffer::channel_rms(ch)) cannot be exposed as object methods; materialize them as eager fields instead (channelRms: Vec<f64> / channelPeak: Vec<f64>, one entry per channel, computed in from_rsac_buffer). Whole-buffer scalar meters likewise become eager f64 fields (rms/peak/rmsDbfs/peakDbfs) computed once from core BEFORE moving samples into Float32Array. SECOND gotcha: bindings/rsac-napi/index.js DESTRUCTURES a FIXED symbol list from nativeBinding and module.exports only {AudioCapture,CaptureTarget,listDevices,getDefaultDevice,platformCapabilities}. Any NEW top-level #[napi] free fn is built into the .node but is UNREACHABLE via 'import {x} from @rsac/audio' unless index.js is also edited. So additive features for the napi binding must hang off an already-exported class (methods on AudioCapture, factories on CaptureTarget) or off fields of an already-returned object - NOT new free functions, when index.js is not in scope to edit. THIRD: u64 counters map to TS via napi BigInt -> 'bigint' (napi-derive-backend PRIMITIVE_TYPES line 95; u64 also -> bigint line 88), and BigInt is gated behind feature napi6 (auto-on via napi9 chain). StreamStats counters MUST be typed bigint in index.d.ts, not number, per the no-lie-typedef rule. CaptureTarget.parse(spec) factory returns Result<Self> mapping rsac::AudioError (InvalidParameter -> ErrorKind::Configuration -> code ERR_RSAC_CONFIGURATION). #[non_exhaustive] rsac::StreamStats can only be built via ::default() in the binding crate (not its defining crate), so tests use Default not struct-literal.

name: macro_export-macro-reexport-via-prelude
description: A #[macro_export] macro is placed ONLY at the crate root, NOT in any module — so 'use rsac::prelude::*' does NOT bring it into scope unless the prelude explicitly re-exports it. To make a macro reachable through a prelude/module, add 'pub use crate::<macro_name>;' to that module (macros are re-exported by path like any item once #[macro_export]ed). A doctest that does 'use rsac::capture;' (explicit crate-root import) will PASS even when the prelude claim is false, masking the gap — so test prelude reachability with 'use rsac::prelude::*;' specifically.

name: ffi-abi-verify-with-nm-T-symbols-not-inspection
description: For FFI->Go (cgo) ABI changes, the definitive check that a new C-header declaration actually links is 'nm librsac_ffi.a | grep <symbol>' showing a 'T' (text/exported) symbol — NOT 'U' (undefined/referenced) and NOT 'r' (read-only metadata like catch_unwind $handlerMap$/$cppxdata$ entries). A header can declare a fn the Rust side forgot to #[no_mangle] pub extern "C"; only the symbol table catches that. The Go static lib is built+copied by bindings/rsac-go/Makefile (cargo build --release in rsac-ffi, then cp target/release/librsac_ffi.a to bindings/rsac-go/lib/). On Windows, 'go test' may fail to LINK the test binary (cgo test-link quirk) even when 'go build ./...' + 'go vet ./...' are green — the canonical CI gate is go build, so don't treat the Windows go-test link error as a regression.
